question: Which organization defined the Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) architecture concept?
options: ETSI (European Telecommunications Standards Institute)
3GPP (3rd Generation Partnership Project)
IEEE (Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers)
IETF (Internet Engineering Task Force)
ITU (International Telecommunication Union)

question: What is Local Breakout (LBO) in the context of MEC?
options: Routing data plane traffic locally to cloud services without crossing the full breadth of the core network
A method for breaking out of a VPN tunnel at the local branch office
A technique that forces all traffic through a centralized security inspection point
A protocol for local AP management without a WLC
A feature that bypasses authentication for trusted local devices
correct_answers: Routing data plane traffic locally to cloud services without crossing the full breadth of the core network

question: Which of the following are key advantages of deploying MEC architecture? Choose all that apply.
options: Enabling URLLC use cases targeting 1ms latency
Reducing backhaul traffic through optimized traffic offload
Better quality of experience (QoE) via edge cache functionality
Eliminating the need for 5G core network functions
Replacing radio access network infrastructure entirely
correct_answers: Enabling URLLC use cases targeting 1ms latency
Reducing backhaul traffic through optimized traffic offload
Better quality of experience (QoE) via edge cache functionality

question: Which MEC design principle states that a MEC application needs to run at the appropriate physical location at any point in time, accounting for compute, storage, network, and latency requirements?
options: Smart application location
NFV alignment
Mobility support
Deployment independence
Application mobility to/from an external system

question: What frequency bands do WLANs operate in? Choose all that apply.
options: 2.4 GHz
5 GHz
6 GHz
700 MHz
28 GHz

question: Which Wi-Fi generation introduced support for the 6 GHz frequency band?
options: Wi-Fi 6E (802.11ax)
Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax without 6 GHz)

question: What is the MAC layer access protocol used in 802.11 WLANs to coordinate transmission among devices sharing the same channel?
options: CSMA/CA (Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Avoidance)
CSMA/CD (Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection)
TDMA (Time Division Multiple Access)
FDMA (Frequency Division Multiple Access)
ALOHA
correct_answers: CSMA/CA (Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Avoidance)

question: What is Split MAC in the context of centralized WLAN architecture?
options: An architecture for the CAPWAP protocol that splits 802.11 functions between the controller-based AP and the WLC
A method of splitting MAC addresses between two APs for load balancing
A security technique that uses two separate MAC addresses for encryption
A feature that splits the MAC layer between wired and wireless segments
A protocol that divides MAC filtering between the RADIUS server and the AP
correct_answers: An architecture for the CAPWAP protocol that splits 802.11 functions between the controller-based AP and the WLC

question: Which of the following functions are handled by the Wireless LAN Controller (WLC) in a centralized WLAN architecture? Choose all that apply.
options: 802.11 association and reassociation (roaming)
Radio frequency (RF) management
Security management
Wireless encryption and decryption of 802.11 frames
Transmission of beacon frames

question: What is Layer 2 intercontroller roaming in a centralized WLAN architecture?
options: The client roams between APs associated with different WLCs but remains on the same VLAN and subnet, keeping its IP address
The client roams between APs and is assigned a new IP address on a different subnet
The client roams between two APs on the same WLC without leaving the building
The client disconnects from Wi-Fi and reconnects to a cellular network
The client moves between two BSSIDs but stays connected to the same AP
correct_answers: The client roams between APs associated with different WLCs but remains on the same VLAN and subnet, keeping its IP address
